title: Golden jubilee celebrations of Gulbarga University Microbiology Department starting on June 25

title: Name, content of NCERT class 6 Kannada textbook turn contentious
content: The recent release of the NCERT class 6 R3 Kannada textbook, controversially titled “Krishna,” has sparked widespread criticism from educationists and writers in Bengaluru.

title: El Nino effect: Centre says 111 districts with ‘poor irrigation facilities’ are a concern
content: The Centre has assessed that 315 districts are vulnerable and 111 districts in 12 States are of primary concern due to poor irrigation facilities

title: Bengaluru Metro Purple Line services resume; technical snag resolved after hours of disruption

content: Commercial kiosks on the walkway would impede pedestrian movement and affect fundamental rights, says court

title: Source of infection of Nipah patient in Kozhikode remains unclear

content: The direction came at the 52nd meeting of the Cauvery Water Management Authority, held amid renewed political debate over the Mekedatu balancing reservoir project

title: Bombay High Court says shared auto not 'workplace' under PoSH Act, sets aside ICC order
content: The Court, in its June 22 order, set aside the findings of an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) that had held a State Bank of India (SBI) employee guilty of sexual harassment based on an incident that occurred during such travel

title: TVK government is clear on political opposition to and administrative cooperation with the Centre, says Vijay
content: He reaffirms his party remains ideologically opposed to the ruling party at the Centre, while making it clear that it would not oppose the Centre blindly

content: The designation of name on the road by the Telangana government, was described as a tribute to the United States and an acknowledgement of Hyderabad’s increasing importance in the U.S.-India relations

title: Bhabanipur poll case: HC judge discloses brother's BJP role; orders CCTV, EVMs preserved
content: Justice Kant directed that the CCTV footage, EVMs and VVPATs will not be erased, overwritten, destroyed, tampered with, transferred, redeployed, opened or dealt with in any manner without the permission of the court

title: Illegal immigrants being deported, genuine Indians have noting to fear: Bengal CM
content: The Chief Minister also said that all those not covered under the Citizenship (Amendment) Act would be sent back

content: Strong concerns were raised over lack of discipline in the State BJP at a meeting convened by party national president Nitin Nabin

title: Indian Navy warships INS Udaygiri, INS Kavaratti in Vietnam to boost maritime cooperation
content: INS Udaygiri, a stealth frigate, and INS Kavaratti, an anti-submarine warfare corvette, are part of the Eastern Fleet and are led by Rear Admiral Alok Ananda, Flag Officer Commanding Eastern Fleet

title: Ayodhya Ram temple donation case: SIT submits preliminary report to U.P. government
content: The Uttar Pradesh government had on June 13 constituted the SIT on a request from the temple trust, following allegations of misappropriation of donations received at the Ayodhya Ram temple

title: Claims about sugarcane juice directly mixed with petrol, car engine absorbing water because of ethanol “misleading, unsubstantiated”: Petroleum Ministry
content: The ministry sought to emphasise that modern vehicles are equipped with design features that prevent entry of water into fuel tanks.

title: West Bengal Govt to bring in law to auction properties of those involved in corruption, says Suvendu
content: Chief Minister  Suvendu Adhikari said that after the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) came to power about 10,000 Bangladeshi nationals have left the State and 1800 are kept in holding centres of the State government across 12 districts.

title: Wang Yi calls for India, China to accelerate resumption of stalled dialogue mechanisms
content: A lengthier Chinese readout of the talks between NSA Ajit Doval and China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi said that the Chinese leader noted that “India is an important neighbour of China”

content: Rudraprayag District Magistrate Vishal Mishra said the matter was resolved through coordinated efforts by the gurdwara administration and the district authorities

content: Sanjay Raut questioned Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis regarding the law and order situation in the State, and dared Sanjay Dina Patil to face him without his police security cover

title: Stretching ‘information’ to include Telegram
content: By reading entire platforms run by companies, which were legal entities by their own nature, under the ambit of ‘information’ under the Act, the government has effectively weaponised the content-blocking provision of Section 69A

content: It comes days after Mr. Tharoor’s remarks on return of normalcy in J&K evoked sharp reactions both from ruling National Conference and the Congress leaders

content: Describing the conviction as politically motivated, AAP Gujarat president Isudan Gadhvi said the party would challenge the verdict before a higher court

content: Supreme Court says petitioners must ideally move Calcutta High Court; plea claims that the outcome of SIR has been linked to food security schemes in the State and could impact welfare benefits

content: Decades ago, the Veeranam project, meant for bringing water to Chennai, had continued to evoke controversy for long

content: The State alone has more than 3,500 millet manufacturing and processing units, making it one of the strongest millet-processing ecosystems in the country, says KAPPEC

title: PM Modi speaks to Qatar’s Amir after 12 Indians killed in Ras Laffan blast, thanks him for condolences
content: Twelve Indians were among 13 people killed in an explosion at an LNG facility in the Ras Laffan Industrial City

content: Passes the interim order pending final determination of a connected case before the Supreme Court and also the reference made to the Larger Bench by a Division Bench of two judges

content: The four-year debenture marks Kaleidofin Capital’s entry into a new pool of international impact capital

title: Watch: HL: Lucknow building fire: FIR cites illegal commercial use | Above the Fold | 23.06.2026

content: Mr. Hazare objected to the hike in fees, saying no rational explanation or financial analysis was provided

title: One-gram gold ring for babies born in T.N. government hospitals
content: Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay will inaugurate the initiative on September 15, the birth anniversary of former Chief Minister C.N. Annadurai

title: GMC Commissioner begins two-day Indore visit to study urban governance, sanitation practices
content: Mayur Ashok reviews the implementation of Swachh Bharat Mission, sanitation management systems, door-to-door waste collection mechanisms, scientific processing of solid waste, resource recovery initiatives and circular economy practices

title: The Goddess of Instant Gratification
content: Was the Nimishamba temple built when the first wave of settlers came? Or was it in the second wave when the company induced them to stay?

title: Ladakh observes shutdown over Centre’s ‘missing’ draft, new policy push
content: ‘The Centre is making an attempt to dilute the commitments,’ says Tsering Dorjey Lakrook, president of the Ladakh Buddhist Association, among the main groups engaging with the Centre over long-pending demands of Statehood, and inclusion in the Sixth Schedule

title: In a first, US Senate votes to halt Trump’s Iran war; President fumes, calls move 'meaningless'
content: In a groundbreaking decision, the US Senate has passed a resolution that requires congressional consent for any further military action against Iran. This marks a pivotal moment in challenging President Trump's military strategy. Although the vote was close and a presidential veto is anticipated, the legislation reflects increasing anxiety regarding the rationale and economic repercussions of the ongoing conflict.

title: Ayodhya Ram temple donation case: SIT recommends Trust revamp; findings may be placed before CM
content: A Special Investigation Team has submitted its initial findings to the Uttar Pradesh government regarding allegations of embezzlement of donations for the Ayodhya Ram temple. The probe, initiated by the temple trust, is actively continuing as investigators gather more information. Officials confirmed the preliminary report was handed over to the Additional Chief Secretary (Home) by the Lucknow Divisional Commissioner, with further details expected as the investigation progresses.

title: Cried out to papa for help: Cabbie spots 10-yr-old sleeping on pavement, abducts, rapes, kills her
content: A father's fragmented memory of a white car and a yellow plate initiated a swift investigation. Investigators pieced together a digital trail, including CCTV footage and cab aggregator data, to track down Basu Kumar Singh, a driver accused of abducting, raping, and murdering a 10-year-old girl. The suspect was apprehended within seven hours of the initial report, later confessing to the heinous crime.

title: 'Ye 12-15 saal khelenge': Goenka's old prediction on Rishabh Pant goes viral
content: Sanjiv Goenka's bold 2025 prediction about Rishabh Pant's future with Lucknow Super Giants has resurfaced after the star wicketkeeper-batter's ₹15 crore trade back to Delhi Capitals. Goenka had envisioned Pant leading LSG to multiple IPL titles over 12-15 years, placing him alongside legends Dhoni and Rohit. However, Pant's two-season stint saw mixed results, culminating in this surprising player swap ahead of IPL 2027.

title: Pune murder plot: Hoodie, Bali getaway, 'snake tale' — Inside the Ketan Agarwal case
content: A real estate businessman's death at Lohagad Fort, initially deemed an accident, was unraveled by CCTV footage showing a suspicious man in a hoodie. Police discovered the victim's fiancée and the hooded man, her alleged lover, conspired to push him to his death. The plot involved multiple attempts, a missing passport, and a motive rooted in a secret relationship and reluctance to elope.

title: Donald Trump regrets selling IBM stock after becoming President
content: US President Donald Trump lauded IBM CEO Arvind Krishna's leadership in semiconductor manufacturing, particularly IBM's quantum computing advancements.  The administration's push for quantum tech, including significant federal funding for IBM's Albany foundry, has boosted the company's stock.  Trump also humorously regretted selling his IBM shares early in his presidency, while his own investment dealings faced scrutiny.

title: Messi and Ronaldo share once-in-a-lifetime World Cup coincidence
content: Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo have reached a remarkable statistical symmetry at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with both stars recording an identical 20-year and 6-day gap between their first and latest tournament goals. Messi moved ahead as the all-time World Cup top scorer with 18 goals, while Ronaldo became the first player to score in six editions of the tournament.

title: No full refund for buyer who defaulted on property sale pact: HC
content: The High Court held that the purchaser himself had defaulted in performing his obligations under the agreement and was therefore entitled only to the amount payable after deduction of 10% of the advance money, as specifically provided under the contract.

title: Oracle layoffs: Company confirms how many employees got 6AM email from leadership
content: Oracle has initiated significant layoffs globally, impacting thousands of employees across US, India, and other regions.  These workforce reductions, attributed to management changes, AI adoption, and strategic shifts, resulted in a 13% drop in its workforce.  The company incurred substantial restructuring costs, with severance packages reportedly less generous than industry peers.  This move comes amidst broader concerns about AI's impact on tech jobs.

title: Rishabh Pant's three-word message to LSG after return to Delhi Capitals
content: Rishabh Pant has returned to Delhi Capitals in a significant trade deal, exchanging places with spinner Kuldeep Yadav who moves to Lucknow Super Giants. Pant's two-year stint with LSG, marked by struggles and disappointing team performances, concluded with a simple "Thank you team" message. This move sees Pant take a pay cut but reunites him with his former franchise after a challenging period.

title: Timer on Earth's plants? New scientific model finally has a timeline
content: In a surprising twist to our understanding of Earth's future, new research suggests that plant life could thrive on our planet for another 1.8 billion years. While the sun’s heat poses a gradual threat, the resilience of the carbon cycle and plant adaptability paints a hopeful picture. Advanced modeling techniques reveal a longer era of lush greenery before extinction eventually takes its toll.

title: After laying off hundreds of engineers, GM now replaces more than 1,000 workers with 50 robots
content: General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry.

title: Indian student headed to US sent back from Amsterdam, unaware his F-1 visa had been revoked
content: An Indian student faced a shock in Amsterdam when US Customs and Border Protection denied him entry to the US, stating his F-1 visa lacked validity.  Despite his university confirming his I-20 and SEVIS record were active, his visa had been revoked months prior.  He was forced to return to India and advised to reapply for a new visa, being transparent about the revocation during the interview.

title: Taylor Swift is reportedly struggling with the Blake Lively fallout
content: Taylor Swift and Blake Lively’s bond has only worsened over the last few months as rumors swirl about the actress being invited to the pop star’s wedding to Travis Kelce which is set to happen in the next few weeks. While Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce focus on their wedding, it seems the pop star has gone through a wave of emotions.

title: The Vikings and Lions are linked to a potential trade for Colts standout
content: The Indianapolis Colts’ star player Anthony Richardson has been actively seeking a trade from the team since February 2026 which has raised eyebrows and sparked chaos about his future in the league. Amid this, the Detroit Lions and the Minnesota Vikings have emerged as potential landing spots for him ahead of the new season.

title: 'Without missiles, we'd be like Gaza': Tehran rejects US calls to limit its defence arsenal
content: Iran's President Pezeshkian has asserted that the missile program is strictly defensive and isn't covered by any agreements with the US. He highlighted its importance for national security, likening Iran's situation to that of Gaza. This declaration surfaces during discussions on a 14-point MoU, which does not include any terms on missile limitations and is centered solely on nuclear arms.

title: Five arrested, boat seized after video of meat, liquor party on Ganga goes viral
content: Varanasi is once again embroiled in controversy after a video surfaced showing men cooking meat and drinking alcohol on a Ganga boat. Police swiftly arrested five individuals, filing charges related to promoting enmity and outraging religious sentiments. This incident follows similar past events involving religious gatherings and alleged disrespect towards the sacred river, highlighting ongoing tensions.

title: 'Next question': Ronaldo refuses journalist's question about Messi
content: Cristiano Ronaldo responded to criticism in emphatic fashion, scoring twice in Portugal’s 5-0 World Cup victory over Uzbekistan to become the first player to score in six different World Cups and Portugal’s all-time leading World Cup goalscorer. The veteran also dismissed retirement talk, declared “I’m back,” and showed little interest in discussing Lionel Messi after the match.

title: Two Kanpur friends who built dreams together die together in Lucknow building fire
content: A devastating fire in Lucknow has shattered two Kanpur families, claiming the lives of close friends and colleagues Sanyam Vij (28) and Surajbhan Singh (25). Both 3D artists were the sole breadwinners, having previously endured personal tragedies. Their untimely deaths have left their families in deep mourning, with funeral arrangements underway for the young men who were dedicated to supporting their loved ones and building a brighter future.

title: UN begins evacuation of 11,000 sailors stranded in Hormuz
content: The UN is actively facilitating the evacuation of approximately 11,000 sailors from the Strait of Hormuz, a critical move emerging from a recent US-Iran peace accord. While maritime activities begin to resume, disputes linger regarding the future management and potential tariffs of the strait's usage. The US upholds its position of global navigation rights, contrasting with Iran's indication that it may resist returning to pre-conflict norms, signaling future negotiations ahead.

title: Ronaldo ends 60-year wait, breaks Portugal World Cup record
content: Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice as Portugal thrashed Uzbekistan 5-0 to strengthen their FIFA World Cup 2026 knockout hopes and rewrite national history. The 41-year-old became Portugal’s all-time leading World Cup goalscorer with 10 goals, surpassing Eusébio’s 60-year-old record, while also extending his own record as the first player to score in six different World Cup tournaments.

title: IAS officer held for siphoning Rs 60 crore from Haryana govt a/cs
content: Haryana's education and agriculture departments saw another IAS officer, Pankaj Aggarwal, arrested in a Rs 60.5 crore funds swindle.  He allegedly approved opening bank accounts and transferred vast sums, violating guidelines. This arrest follows that of former Panchkula municipal commissioner RK Singh, with both cases linked to a larger Rs 593 crore scam across eight state departments. The CBI is investigating the alleged siphoning of funds through shell entities.

title: Major fire breaks out in Delhi labour settlement near Udyog Bhawan, no injuries reported
content: A fire erupted in labour slums near Delhi's Udyog Bhawan on Wednesday, originating from an electric panel and spreading to temporary structures. Around 20 fire tenders battled the blaze, bringing it under control by 5:30 am. No casualties have been reported. This incident follows a major slum fire behind Maulana Azad Medical College days earlier, which involved residential areas and storage facilities, including plywood, leading to rapid fire spread.

title: Pune realtor murder: Fiancée, lover arrested for pushing him off cliff; CCTV unravel conspiracy
content: A Pune realtor's staged accidental fall from Lohagad Fort was unraveled by CCTV footage showing a man in a hoodie trailing the victim. Investigators discovered the fiancée, Siya Goyal, was in a relationship with Chetan Chaudhary, who allegedly pushed the realtor off a cliff. The couple, arrested for murder and conspiracy, had reportedly made prior attempts to orchestrate a fatal accident.

title: Mac Jones finally breaks silence on San Francisco 49ers trade drama with bold admission
content: Mac Jones has finally addressed the growing trade rumors surrounding his future with the San Francisco 49ers. Speaking on The Rich Eisen Show, Jones made his feelings clear about staying in San Francisco while also admitting that anything can happen before the trade deadline. With a strong 2025 campaign behind him and several quarterback-needy teams keeping tabs, his future remains one of the biggest storylines heading into the 2026 NFL season

title: 'I will take the guarantee of Kohli, Rohit': Ex-India star makes bold 2027 ODI WC claim
content: Former India cricketer Mohammad Kaif has backed Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma to play in the 2027 ODI World Cup, citing Rohit's fitness, passion and improved batting approach. Kaif also believes India will be favourites for the tournament, with most members of the 2023 World Cup squad, including Bumrah, Gill, Rahul, Iyer and Kuldeep, expected to remain part of the team.

title: Delhi Capitals cricketer accused of sexual relationship under false promise of marriage
content: Bengal and Delhi Capitals cricketer Abishek Porel faces serious allegations after a woman filed a police complaint accusing him of a broken promise of marriage, assault, and criminal intimidation. The woman claims a three-year relationship soured last year, with Porel allegedly backing out of marriage plans. Porel has vehemently denied the accusations, calling them baseless and suggesting they are timed with his recent cricketing success. An investigation is underway.

title: FIFA WC: Ronaldo scripts history in Portugal's dominant 5-0 victory over Uzbekistan
content: Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice as Portugal crushed Uzbekistan 5-0 in Houston to strengthen their place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 knockout stages. The captain opened the scoring before adding a record-breaking second goal, becoming the first player to score in six consecutive World Cups and surpassing Eusébio as Portugal’s all-time leading World Cup scorer.

title: GE sends AI 171 engine analysis, final AAIB report likely in 2 months
content: GE Aerospace has sent the analysis reports for the engines of AI171 that had crashed in Ahmedabad last June, killing 260 people. All eyes are now on the Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau. "Air accident investigations have to zero in on what could have happened.

title: "Heart Found Its Home": Pune Woman's Romantic Posts With Fiance Before Killing Him
content: On May 19, Siya had posted her 'birthday countdown' story on her social media as she and Ketan danced and twirled to a romantic song.

title: Viral Video Of Chicken, Beer Party On Ganga Boat Lands 5 In Jail In Varanasi
content: Five people were arrested after a video allegedly showing them cooking chicken and consuming beer on a boat in the middle of the Ganga river in Varanasi went viral on social media, a police officer said on Tuesday.

title: Is BEd Still Relevant? Experts Say CBSE's AI Move May Rewrite Teaching Jobs
content: Education experts say AI will not replace teachers. But it could widen the gap between teachers who adapt and those who do not.

content: Since fleeing India, Nirav Modi has faced extradition proceedings, asset seizures and multiple recovery actions by banks and enforcement agencies.

content: Sergio Gor underscored Washington's commitment to strengthening cooperation with India and suggested that US President Donald Trump attached significant importance to the relationship.

title: Honey-Trap, Suicide Attack Angles Emerge In Jaipur Woman's Jaish Links Probe
content: According to sources familiar with the investigation, Babita had allegedly been in contact with Pakistan-based handlers linked to the terror outfit for nearly two years.

title: Fuel Ready, But Not Pumps: The E100 Challenge
content: Ethanol production capacity has expanded to nearly 2,000 crore litres annually, exceeding the requirements of the E20 programme.

title: What Is Driving Europe's Heatwave?
content: Europe Heatwave: Scientists say there has been an increase in high pressure systems in Europe in recent decades but whether this is a consequence of climate change remains a subject of debate.

title: Only 24% Of Americans Think War With Iran Was Worth It: Polls
content: Only 23% of Americans — including just half of Republicans — think the US is now in a stronger position with Iran compared with its position before the war, the poll found.

title: Kim Jong Un Unveils Plans For 10,000-Ton Warships, Nuclear Navy
content: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un said his country was "equipping the Navy with nuclear weapons" as he commissioned a warship, state media reported on Wednesday.

title: Would "Cancel Meetings" With Iran If UN Nuclear Inspections Denied: Trump
content: Responding to questions on UN nuclear watchdog inspectors would be deployed under the agreement, Trump insisted that Iran had already agreed in principle.

title: Trump Claims Record 19 Million Barrels Of Oil Transited Hormuz; Prices Ease
content: Oil prices ticked lower Tuesday with Brent crude futures, the international benchmark, fell 45 cents to USD 77.45 a barrel. West Texas Intermediate futures declined 34 cents to USD 73.52.

title: Trains Halted Across Germany After Radio System Outage
content: Germany's national railway operator said it halted trains across the country on Tuesday evening because of a problem with a communication system.

title: US Senate Votes To Halt Iran War In Rebuke To Trump
content: The US Senate backed legislation on Tuesday directing President Donald Trump to halt US military action against Iran, the latest rebuke of the Republican president from an increasingly restive Congress.

title: A tale of two deals: Trump's 'infinity' inspections meets Iran's 'not so fast'
content: the peace agreement purportedly pounded out during talks in the Alps has more holes than Swiss cheese . one side believes it has come away with a deal, the other is still dealing with the idea . the other side believes that the deal is going well . it turns out that the pact sounded suspiciously like a completed real-estate closing .

title: Fields of work by foreign-funded NGOs outlined, proselytisation excluded
content: Bharti Jain is senior editor with The Times of India, New Delhi. She has been writing on security matters since 1996. Having covered the Union home ministry, security agencies, Election Commission and the ‘prime’ political beat, the Congress, for The Economic Times all these years, she moved to TOI in August 2012. Her repertoire of news stories delves into the whole gamut of issues related to terrorism and internal strife, besides probing strategic affairs in India’s neighbourhood. Read More

title: Flu kills 1.2 lakh/year in India, senior citizens bear the brunt
content: Anuja Jaiswal is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India, with an impressive 18-year career in narrative journalism. She specializes in health and heritage reporting, expertly simplifying complex health information to make it engaging and understandable for readers. Her deep dives into heritage topics are well-researched, resulting in captivating narratives that resonate with her audience. Over the years, she has worked in Chandigarh, Chhattisgarh and West UP, gaining diverse on-ground experience that shapes her storytelling. Read More

title: Guwahati dropped from Japan PM’s itinerary; summit shifted to Delhi
content: Prabin Kalita is a journalist at The Times of India and is currently the Chief of Bureau (northeast). He has been reporting in mainstream Indian national media since 2001. He has been a field journalist reporting gamut of issues from India’s northeastern region and major developments in neighbouring countries like Myanmar, China, Bhutan and Bangladesh concerning India and northeastern region. He has been covering insurgency—internal and cross-border, politics, natural calamities, environment etc. He is a post-graduate in Geological Sciences from Gauhati University. Read More
